description: Causal-flow hub test in bf16: for each candidate component swap its = output with its single-operator (b*c=) value and measure how much the BODMAS-LTR logit gap collapses, ranking the true causal carrier; compare pre-FT vs post-FT.
motivation: Test whether L22 is really the hub, and whether the hub is staged-computation or a transition point - in unquantized bf16 across models.
formal_results: Qwen-7B pre-FT hub = MLP23 (+ L20H10), NOT L22 (clean gap 0.375). Post-FT the carrier SHIFTS to L22 attention (attn22h13/h10 collapse 5.6 of an 8.6 gap). L27H11 is top-10 but comparable to L20H10, not strictly dominant.
results_description: In bf16 the base hub is MLP23 + L20H10; L22 attention becomes the hub only after fine-tuning. The notebook 'L22 hub' was a 4-bit artifact.
conclusions: L22 is the TRANSITION layer, not the staged-multiply or lookup layer; the single-layer framing conflated causal importance with computational content.
